title: Refactor a two-step `with` into pattern-matched `case`
prompt: A `with` expression is overkill for a single clause. Refactor `def get_user(id) do with {:ok, user} <- Repo.fetch(User, id), do: {:ok, user} end` into a direct call `def get_user(id), do: Repo.fetch(User, id)`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Users do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Single-clause `with` → direct call. One canonical answer.
research_citation: lucasvegi/Elixir-Refactorings: 'Remove redundant last clause in with'
